---
title: 'Small Wins'
date: '2016-03-21T12:17:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1009002777177446496'
blogger_filename: '/2016/03/small-wins.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Recovery'
  - 'Renewing the Mind (Stinking Thinking)'
---

<br /><br /><h3>I had the Check Engine light go off on the car.&nbsp;</h3><div><br /></div><div>That, alone, has been enough to put me into a funk for weeks. I breathed, tried not to panic.<br /><br /><a href="http://www.oreillyauto.com/site/c/home.oap">O'Reilly Auto</a> Parts pulled the code, and it was a Thermostat ($30). Most shops wanted $200 for the repair (including parts), but YouTube made it look like I could do it myself.<br /><br />It's estimated to take a professional mechanic 1.5 hours, it took me 4 hours. Yeah! I did it.<br /><br />I didn't even kick and scream and yell once. I did loose a tool in the engine area... but I stayed calm, and got it out, and continued.<br /><br />Celebrating the small wins tonight.<br /><br />Darrell</div>
